desc:
Carved from the living rock of Zirakzigil, this small tower has stood since the
age when dwarves first delved beneath these peaks. The thousands of years that
have passed have done nothing to mar the elegant stonework, and, much like a
dwarf, this tower stands mute and resolute - stoically resisting whatever fury
the mountains can unleash upon it. A solitary window looks out onto a small
field of snow, here at the top of the world above even the clouds.
name: Durin's Tower

desc:
This small and snow-covered plain lies atop Celebdil, the Silvertine. To the
south is Durin's Tower - a simple stone edifice that somehow seems the height of
Dwarven elegance as it might be part of the mountain itself. In every other
direction, the clouds obscure the lands like a great grey blanket - only the
most severe of storms can hurl snow at this height, but once it falls, it cannot
melt. Some distance away, the equally impressive peaks of Caradhras and
Fanuidhol protrude above the eternal cloud cover, forming the remainder of the
majestic triumvirate of peaks over Khazad-dum.
name: Upon the Peak of Zirakzigil

desc:
The damp mist enfolds you as you begin to plummet towards the distant earth. You
break through the bottom of the clouds and are rewarded with a brief but
spectacular view of the surrounding lands, but the onrushing ground quickly
squelches your sense of wonder.
name: Above the Clouds

desc:
The damp mist enfolds you as you begin to plummet towards the distant earth. You
break through the bottom of the clouds and are rewarded with a brief but
spectacular view of the surrounding lands, but the onrushing ground quickly
squelches your sense of wonder.
name: Above the Clouds

desc:
The stair has reached one end of a relatively long straight stretch, which is a
pleasant change after hundreds of twisting steps. It might be the eastern end,
but there is no quick way to be certain - the many sharp and irregular turnings
in the rest of the stairwell make it extremely easy to lose the sense of
direction.
name: Hidden Stair

desc:
The stairwell, which, above, is extremely contorted, abruptly stops bending
right in this place. The dazzling convolutions give way to a long straight ramp,
which gradually slopes down into the darkness. Almost no noise, apart from that
of your own making, can be heard here: massive layers of rock separate this
place from the rest of the world.
name: Hidden Stair

desc:
Bending alternately clockwise and anticlockwise, with no semblance of order, the
stair continues its long journey through the hard rock. The continuous bends
have a somewhat hypnotic effect, reinforced by the fact that the walls are so
close to each other that one has almost no freedom to move: the only choice lies
between going ahead or turning back.
name: Hidden Stair

desc:
The stairwell, enclosed within massive, cracked stone walls, seems to get
narrower and narrower as it descends, until it becomes barely wide enough to let
a single person through. A thin layer of dust, which has accumulated with the
passage of time, rests on the barely worn steps.
name: Hidden Stair

desc:
The upper end of an old stone stair lies a few paces east of here, where the
narrow stairwell opens into a small secret chamber. In the opposite direction
the stair plunges down very steeply, and the rough stone walls surrounding it
grow nearer to each other. There seem to be little signs of passage here: in
contrast with some larger tunnels, the floor and the steps have not been worn
out by thousands and thousands of moving feet.
name: Top of a Hidden Stair

desc:
This small room is enclosed by roughly formed walls. They block any movement
south or east, but west a passageway leads between them. The northern wall is
smooth and well fit, revealing a moveable slab. The disuse of this room is
evident, from not only the stale air and dust, but also from the cobwebs forming
a silky screen over much of the walls.
name: Small Alcove

desc:
This unbroken spiral of steps - assumed to exist only in legend even by the
Dwarves - climbs ever onward through the heart of Zirakzigil. The steps are cut
for Dwarven legs, making travel for the taller folk an arduous task. Far below,
darkness floods the shaft and conceals whatever long-lost secrets that lie
below.
name: The Endless Stair

desc:

below.
name: The Endless Stair

desc:
A cool breeze drifts lazily down from above, where the stair appears to come
finally to an end. The dark, stale atmosphere from below seems to fight to gain
supremacy here, at the top of the stair, and dust motes dance and twirl around,
before falling in a soft carpet on the cold, smooth stone. The walls and floor
here are perfectly polished, and their craftsmanship unmarked by the long
passage of time.
name: Beneath the Tower

desc:
Less of this avenue has collapsed than to the east and west, probably because
fewer rooms were excavated to the north and south. But the pavement is buckled
downward in great cracked dips, and large slabs of the floor and wall lie in the
roadway and must be climbed over. The air here is very stale; it seems as if no
living thing has come here for centuries.
name: Avenue

desc:
This broad avenue, once lined by shops and residences carved into the stone, has
been almost completely obliterated. Rock pressing down from above has crushed
the chambers and corridors, squeezing them flat and filling them with massive
stone slabs. However, along the northern edge of the avenue, a small remnant of
the roadway remains, virtually a crawlway about four feet high at most whose
roof is composed of the slanted slabs of the fallen ceiling.
name: Collapsed Avenue
